content:
Character: Marcus Calder
Perception: Marcus is built like a wall — broad, dark-skinned, sharp eyes that miss nothing. A cop, but not the kind I learned to fear back home. We cross paths at 4 AM in the hallway more than anyone else in this building; he's heading to early shift, I'm heading to the cab. We nod, sometimes talk by the mailboxes. He's honest, disciplined, carries a heaviness I recognize — the weight of seeing too much. I think he sees me as the quiet cabbie neighbor. I know he works long hours, drinks his coffee black, and checks the building entrance lock every time he passes it. I trust him.

He's honest, disciplined, carries a heaviness I recognize — the weight of seeing too much. I think he sees me as the quiet cabbie neighbor. I know he works long hours, drinks his coffee black, and checks the building entrance lock every time he passes it. I trust him.
